ocr.text: The dairy sector remains a cornerstone of the agricultural landscape in Europe, with production volumes reflecting both
regional demand and global market trends. As indicated in the accompanying data table, the total dairy production is
categorized into various segments, including milk and cream, which are critical for maintaining a balanced supply chain.
Notably, cream's contribution to the overall production volume is significant, with its 200M category reaching a notable 45M.
Furthermore, the total dairy output is reflected in the metrics for milk, which is reported as TotalDairy, demonstrating the
comprehensive scale of dairy production. In the cheese segment, the 200M category alone has recorded a substantial 90M,
underlining the importance of cheese in European diets and its economic impact. Similarly, butter production, categorized under
the 15M column, has reached 4M, indicating a steady demand for dairy fats.
ProductCategory  Revenue(CHF)  NetIncome(CHF)  Volume(liters)  DairyProducts  ProductionCosts(CHF)
Cheese  80M  15M  200M  40M  10M  120M  30M  7M  [HIDDEN]
Yogurt  70M  12M  150M  35M  8M  100M  20M  5M  80M
Butter  60M  10M  120M  25M  6M  75M  15M  [HIDDEN]  65M
Cream  50M  8M  100M  20M  5M  [HIDDEN]  10M  3M  [HIDDEN]
[HIDDEN]  40M  6M  80M  15M  4M  30M  8M  2M  35M
[HIDDEN]  450M  81M  1.1B  210M  58M  675M  [HIDDEN]  41M  440M
ExportVolume(liters)  100M  30M  50M  20M  10M  15M  8M  5M  12M
DomesticVolume(liters)  400M  70M  150M  40M  5M  30M  12M  3M  28M
The ice cream sector, another vital component of dairy consumption, is represented under the Milk column as IceCream, further
emphasizing the diverse applications of dairy products. Overall, the TotalDairy column captures the aggregate output
effectively, with 50M reflecting a robust domestic volume of 155M. Such metrics not only highlight the current production
landscape but also serve as a basis for future growth strategies within the dairy industry across Europe.
